name: Rokid Zoom Camera
summary: Unofficial Rokid Glasses camera prototype with temple-swipe zoom and physical-button capture
description: Rokid Zoom Camera is an unofficial glasses-side camera prototype that keeps the UI minimal while adding stepped zoom control from temple swipes. It captures zoomed photos and zoomed videos with the physical Rokid button and saves media locally through Android MediaStore.
author: tenru-do

listing.descriptionMarkdown:
# About
Rokid Zoom Camera is an unofficial camera prototype for Rokid Glasses. It is designed for quick zoomed capture with a small, dim preview that avoids filling the glasses display.

# Key features
- Camera2 preview on Rokid Glasses
- Temple swipe forward or backward to step zoom in or out
- Physical button short press captures a zoomed photo
- Physical button long press starts or stops zoomed video recording
- Small red REC indicator while recording
- Saves photos and videos locally to DCIM/Camera through Android MediaStore
- Does not use cloud APIs or upload captured media

# Controls
- Temple swipe forward: zoom in
- Temple swipe backward: zoom out
- Physical button short press: take a photo, or stop video if already recording
- Physical button long press: start or stop video recording

# Notes
This is a debug-signed prototype and is not an official Rokid app. Some behavior depends on firmware internals observed during device testing, including Rokid button broadcasts and a temporary official-camera setting override. Compatibility can vary by model or firmware. The upstream project currently has no open-source license, so treat the source as all rights reserved unless the author publishes a license.

releases.notes: Initial prototype APK. Adds Camera2 preview, stepped temple-swipe zoom, physical-button photo capture, physical-button video recording, local DCIM/Camera saves, and a minimal low-brightness glasses UI.
changes: Stepped zoom controlled by temple swipe.
Zoomed photo capture with the physical button.
Zoomed video recording with REC indicator.
Local media saves through Android MediaStore.
